qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: DOM XSS via unsanitized options in output() document.write calls
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns jsPDF output() HTML generation and document.write() injection, not addImage BMP parsing, BMPDecoder, or unvalidated BMP header dimensions.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of service via unbounded memory allocation in PNG image decoding
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding shares jsPDF addImage and image-dimension DoS context, but it identifies the PNG decoder path in src/modules/png_support.js using PNG IHDR dimensions, not the advisory-described BMPDecoder path and BMP width/height header handling.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: malformed BMP dimensions flowing through addImage into BMPDecoder and causing excessive allocation. The closest finding is a separate PNG decoder dimension-allocation issue, which is only a surface-level similarity.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-95fx-jjr5-f39c describes DoS from harmful BMP files with large width and/or height entries reaching jsPDF addImage and BMPDecoder.
dos-via-unbounded-png-image reports processPNG(), processAlphaPNG(), processIndexedPNG(), fast-png, and PNG IHDR dimensions in src/modules/png_support.js.
dom-xss-via-output-document-write reports output() document.write() injection in src/jspdf.js and has no overlap with BMP parsing or image-dimension allocation.
